blocks.content: Thursday after school. Side exit near the gym — the one I'd been using since the first week because it let me avoid the main crowd.
I came through the door and he was already there. Not alone — two guys I didn't recognize standing behind him like punctuation.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: Thought you'd sneak out the side.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: Then why you always taking the long way?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: It's not the long way. It's just a way.
The two guys behind him didn't say anything. They didn't need to. They were there to make the number different.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: You were watching when I talked to Michaels. Like you had something to say.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: You didn't have to. You had that look.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: Like you think you're better than me because you run with Michaels.
The street behind me was empty. Jonathan was still inside at practice. The two guys hadn't moved.
